title: Route traffic with an Ingress
scenario: You have two Services already running: `frontend-svc` on port 80 and `api-svc` on port 8080. Currently the starter Ingress routes all traffic to `frontend-svc`. Add a second rule so that requests to path `/api` are forwarded to `api-svc` instead.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: app-ingress
  namespace: default
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  rules:
  - http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: frontend-svc
            port:
              number: 80

objectives.description: Ingress has a rule for path `/` pointing to `frontend-svc` on port 80
Ingress has a rule for path `/api` pointing to `api-svc` on port 8080

hints.text: You need to add a second entry under `spec.rules[0].http.paths`. Copy the existing path block and change `path`, `service.name`, and `service.port.number`.
The new path entry should have `path: /api`, `pathType: Prefix`, and route to `api-svc` on port `8080`.
Paths are matched in order. Make sure `/api` appears before `/` if your ingress controller does exact-first matching — or use `pathType: Exact` for the API route.

solution:
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: app-ingress
  namespace: default
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  rules:
  - http:
      paths:
      - path: /api
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: api-svc
            port:
              number: 8080
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: frontend-svc
            port:
              number: 80
